title: Arkhaven Medical Platforms
preview: Background: Arkhaven runs a clinical triage + patient Q&A layer across several hospital networks. Currently on Redwood Hosted API with steady growth (1.2B tokens/mo). Primary drivers for expansion: predictable latency for emergent triage workflows, data residency for EMR-integrated sites, and unit-cost reduction for...
text:
Background: Arkhaven runs a clinical triage + patient Q&A layer across several hospital networks. Currently on Redwood Hosted API with steady growth (1.2B tokens/mo). Primary drivers for expansion: predictable latency for emergent triage workflows, data residency for EMR-integrated sites, and unit-cost reduction for large-batch summarization pipelines.

Commercial ask: 1) Pilot Dedicated (3x A100-equivalent pool) for latency-critical triage and trial matching; 2) Adopt Redwood Optimize for batching, prefix/KV caching, and quantization recommendations to reduce token spend and GPU-hours; 3) Add optional Private (on-prem package) for two hospital partners in Midwest with air-gapped requirements.

Security: Requested SSO/SAML, KMS integration (customer-managed key), SOC2 + ISO27001 evidence. Legal requested a KMS amendment and data residency clause for on-prem sites. Security already con...

text:
Summary: Large financial analytics provider building RAG over internal research + regulatory filings. Target index 200M–800M vectors initially; plan growth to 1B+ within 12–18 months. Primary workload: embeddings + two-stage rerank (ANN -> cross-attention reranker).

Key constraints: cannot send PII or regulated doc content to third-party public cloud. Customer wants Private deployment or on-prem with customer-managed KMS and full audit logging. Enterprise SLAs required.

Performance & cost targets: p95 rerank latency <=150ms, p99 <=300ms. Throughput target 1,000 qps peak (avg 250 qps). Cost sensitivity: unit cost target $0.0005–$0.002 per query depending on rerank depth; looking to minimize GPU token cost via batching, prefix/KV caching and 8-bit quantization.

POC scope: ingest 50M vectors subset -> validate ANN sharding, hybrid routing, and rerank latency at target QPS; run A/B aga...

title: Axion Sprout
preview: Founders building a conversational fintech assistant for SMB accounting customers. Primary motivators: low-latency chat for agents (<150ms p95 ideal), high-quality embeddings for invoice/search, and deterministic reranking for suspicious tx alerts. Starting with hosted API to iterate; 'VPC-curious' once usage grows....
text: Founders building a conversational fintech assistant for SMB accounting customers. Primary motivators: low-latency chat for agents (<150ms p95 ideal), high-quality embeddings for invoice/search, and deterministic reranking for suspicious tx alerts. Starting with hosted API to iterate; 'VPC-curious' once usage grows. Early security asks: SOC2 snapshot, SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration. Cost-sensitive but willing to commit if latency and reliability stable. Quote from CEO: "We need predictable latency — not just average, p95 matters for UX." Trial shows sporadic p95 spikes during bursts; customer logged sample trace ids. They submitted Fireflies transcript for intro call. Wants a short eval matrix to compare Redwood hosted vs competitors on latency/cost. AE recommended Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching and cache.

Lead: Nina Park (CTO). Small devtools startup building an IDE-integrated code assistant + repo search. Wants Hosted API (self-serve) — fast onboarding is key. Primary asks: 1) long context support (claims they need ~64k token windows for whole-repo snippets), 2) clear rate limit tiers and burst behavior (expected low QPS but spiky during CI/IDE events), 3) example SDK snippets for Node + Python showing streaming + function calling for code completions, 4) deterministic cost estimates for batching files and embeddings.

Recent call notes:
- "We need to surface whole-file context and preserve cross-file references when generating completions." — Nina.
- They ran a quick PoC against competitor X (small window) and want to compare latency and cost on longer contexts.
- Latency SLAs: interactive in-editor <300ms for short prompts; acceptable up to ~1s for heavy context completion. Throughp...

text:
Summary: mid-market SaaS for customer support teams. Pushing knowledge-base RAG into support UI (in-app answer suggestions + agent assist). Primary asks: high QPS (sustained ~200-300 qps), predictable per-query cost, and sub-150ms 95th latency for top-k rerank flow. Currently prototyping with third-party vector store + OpenAI embeddings; want single vendor for hosted embedding + rerank to simplify SLAs and lower egress/cost.

Key customer constraints (from calls):
- Index size: ~6-12M doc chunks (moderate scale).
- Query pattern: short queries, many concurrent users, spikes during support events.
- Cost sensitivity: wants per-query model to be ~<$0.006 median to justify replacing current provider.
- Security: SSO (SAML), SOC2 required, audit logs + configurable retention. Data residency for EU customers (subset of customers).

Quote from Head of ML: "If we can get reliable 95th p95 la...

Network specifics captured duri...
text:
Prospect: regional fintech (payments + risk). Very strict data residency requirements (primary in DE + FR). Primary ask: Redwood Private deployed into Archway-managed VPC with no public egress, direct connectivity (AWS PrivateLink or peering/TGW), and HSM-backed KMS for key material.

Network specifics captured during workshop: prefer PrivateLink for cross-account access but networking team concerned about endpoints per AZ and cost at scale. Alternative: VPC peering via Transit Gateway with strict route table controls. Must support IP allowlists, per-subnet firewall rules, and ability to lock down egress to a small set of controlled NAT/egress proxies.

Security review status: questionnaire 70% complete. Pending items: HSM/KMS key custody model confirmation (customer-managed CMKs vs Redwood-managed), audit_log retention window (they require 7 years for some flows), and proof of no mod...

text:
Summary: mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood for embeddings + semantic search. Started with Hosted API (fast test), moving to Dedicated for predictable throughput and lower unit cost. Team: CTO (Anya Venkatar), Head of Search Eng (Caleb Moore), Data Eng lead (Rina Patel).

POC scope and ask: 2-week hosted-API POC to validate embedding quality + ingestion friction. Follow-on Dedicated trial to run throughput tests and pricing bake. Key metrics: embedding ingestion throughput (batch and streaming), end-to-end query latency (target <80ms for top-k retrieval), nightly batch job ingest 1M rows within a 3-hour window, cost per 1M embeddings. Vector DB integration must support hybrid search and similarity+filtering by metadata.

- Currently using Postgres + pgvector for small workloads; testing Pinecone demo for latency. Want to avoid re-architecting i...

text:
- Company: fleet-scale mobility platform (ride-hail + logistics). Very cost sensitive at scale; forecasting heavy peak windows (08:00–10:00, 17:00–19:00 local).
- Primary workloads: conversational in-car assistant (short multi-turn chat), route re-ranking for ETA/traffic, and rapid summarization of driver reports.
- Latency targets: p50 < 30ms, p95 <= 120ms end-to-end for chat assistant (edge device + service). Throughput target: sustained 4k–6k req/s during peak, spiky bursts up to 12k req/s.
- Current stack: self-hosted on mixed GPU fleet (A100 and older T4s); using naive batching. Unit cost is high, and cold-starts hurt SLAs.
- Requirements called out repeatedly on calls: VPC/private control plane, KMS integration, audit logs with 1y retention, and EU data residency for certain accounts.
- Interest areas with Redwood: intelligent dynamic batching with latency SLOs, prefix/KV cachin...

text:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS (B2B support automation) evaluating Hosted -> Dedicated. Trial showed product-fit for RAG-enabled support agent + doc search. Primary concern: predictable latency at peak and EU residency.

Trial observations (hosted):
- Duration: 30 days. Tokens used: ~310k.
- Avg requests per minute (trial avg): 420 rpm (~7 QPS).
- Peak observed: 4,800 rpm (~80 QPS) during support surge window (Tue 14:00-15:00 ET).
- Typical concurrency: 60 concurrent sessions baseline, spikes to ~180 during incidents.
- Token mix observed: ~30% short (<64 tokens), ~50% medium (64-512), ~20% long (512-1500). Prompts avg ~140 tokens, responses avg ~360 tokens => ~500 tokens/request.

Requirements stated by customer:
- P99 latency target: <350ms for responses up to 512 tokens; degrade gracefully for long generations.
- Throughput: support 80 QPS sustained for 10-minute windows; handle 180 conc...

CTO quote on 2026-02-14 call: "we need p99 under 500ms for chat routes or we can't go...
text:
POC summary: Foxglove is evaluating Redwood Dedicated for low-latency chat + reranking across high-volume user traffic. Primary goal is a predictable Dedicated cluster with throughput guarantees and explicit latency SLOs per route. CTO quote on 2026-02-14 call: "we need p99 under 500ms for chat routes or we can't go live".

POC scope and acceptance criteria (short):
- Sustained throughput: 500 qps of short-chat requests (avg 40 tokens) with spikes to 1k qps for 2-minute bursts.
- Latency SLOs: p95 < 200ms, p99 < 500ms for chat API; reranking p95 < 120ms.
- Soak acceptance: 48-hour steady-state run at 350 qps with no SLO violations > 1% and no memory leaks.
- Throughput guarantee: reserve GPU pool sized to meet steady-state + 2x burst headroom; explicit failover to regional backup pool.

Test plan executed so far (high level):
- 2026-02-10: kickoff; drive doc with representative prompt...

text:
Summary: Indigo AgentFlow builds a hybrid agent-assist + automation layer for mid-market retail and fintech contact centers. Primary interest is robust tool-calling — create/update Zendesk tickets, issue refunds via Stripe API, trigger PagerDuty escalations, and open engineering tickets in JIRA when automated remediation fails.

Customer quotes / voice: 
- "We need the model to make outbound API calls the same way a human agent would — but fail safely. No blind refunds." — Head of Ops
- "Top priority is predictable behavior during holiday spikes; last year our webhook throttles collapsed." — CTO

Technical requirements (callouts):
- Tool-calling surface: create_ticket, update_ticket, add_comment, issue_refund, escalate_to_pagerduty, create_jira_issue. Idempotency keys required for all mutating calls.
- Integrations: Zendesk (sandbox + prod), Stripe (restricted test keys, PCI scope red...

text: Lead type: inbound SMB B2B SaaS. Founder CTO: Evan Martinez. Primary use: in‑app chat assistant for their B2B product (support + workflow helper). Self‑serve signup happened 2026-02-10; already integrated client-side streaming SDK in prototype. Key ask: 'we need streaming that doesn't add >200ms p95 on token delivery' (quote from Evan). Prefer hosted API for speed to market; will evaluate Dedicated later only if cost at scale justifies. Model preferences: open weights (7B/13B) or Redwood-curated low-latency variants; fallback routing to 7B for burst. Cost sensitivity: high — unit economics must allow $0.005-0.01 per active user per month at pilot scale. Security: require SSO (SAML) for admin portal, audit logs retention for 90 days, SOC2 evidence for procurement. Integration notes: using React web client, websockets for streaming, currently prototyping SSE; want sample code showing st...

Account summary: biotech mid-market with heavy regulatory posture. High interest in private deployments for PHI flows. Long POC cycle expected because models will be used for clinical memo summarization and lit review.

Timeline (recent):
- 2025-11-12: Intro call (AE + CTO) — demo of hosted API. CTO liked latency profile; asked about on-prem options.
- 2025-12-02: SE session (Sofia) went deep on embeddings pipeline; customer asked about deterministic reranking and vector store compatibility.
- 2026-01-14: Product demo recorded (Fireflies ff-2026-01-14-ct-demo). Follow-up asked for security deck and HSM/KMS flow.
- 2026-01-28: Security handoff meeting — security team requested SOC2/ISO evidence + architecture diagram. Provided security FAQ link.

text:
Account summary:
- Large enterprise financial services org. Evaluating Redwood for a mix of internal assistant + customer support summarization + doc search. Strong preference for VPC/Private deployment (hosted acceptable for early evaluation only).

Key requirements (why this deal is in the SDK parity path):
- Streaming reliability is a must-have. They see frequent mid-stream disconnects behind corporate proxies and want predictable client behavior:
 - Clear surfaced error when the stream breaks (include request_id and partial output context).
 - Guidance on what is safe to retry vs not safe to retry ("we cannot double-send an action" / avoid duplicate side effects).
 - Prefer that SDKs do NOT automatically retry mid-stream; instead expose enough metadata for app-level retries w/ idempotency.
- Strict timeout expectations:
 - They enforce hard end-to-end request budgets at their gate...

title: Elmwood Lynx Search
preview: Summary: Elmwood Lynx is a mid-market SaaS analytics company building semantic search into their product. Primary workloads: embeddings for nightly batch jobs (50k+ docs/day), low-latency semantic search for product UI (target 95th pct < 200ms), and occasional reranking for recommendations. Currently using a Postgre...
text:
Summary: Elmwood Lynx is a mid-market SaaS analytics company building semantic search into their product. Primary workloads: embeddings for nightly batch jobs (50k+ docs/day), low-latency semantic search for product UI (target 95th pct < 200ms), and occasional reranking for recommendations. Currently using a Postgres/pgvector prototype but concerned about scale and cost. Team prefers starting on Hosted API (fast onboarding) with plan to move to Dedicated once throughput and residency controls are validated.

POC specifics: ran a hosted-based POC ingest of 48k documents via Airflow -> S3 -> Redwood batch embedding jobs. Evaluated two vector DB flows: managed Pinecone (fast index ops, easier ops) vs self-managed Milvus (cheaper at scale but ops-heavy). Redwood hosted kept KV/cache behavior good during prefix-heavy query patterns; observed 95th pct latency ~120ms on demo stress test (sin...

title: Driftwood Voice Systems
preview:
Customer wanted turnkey voice embedding + streaming generative responses.

Economic concern: continuous 24/7 model use; downstream costs matter (transcription + tokens).

Security: wanted SOC2 + ability to restrict data retention per customer. Our private offering seen as heavy lift.

Quote from Samir: 'We need some...
text:
Customer wanted turnkey voice embedding + streaming generative responses.

Economic concern: continuous 24/7 model use; downstream costs matter (transcription + tokens).

Security: wanted SOC2 + ability to restrict data retention per customer. Our private offering seen as heavy lift.

Quote from Samir: 'We need something that devs can spin up with minimal ops overhead — brand matters.'

SE note: POC exposed slightly higher p95 for short-turn interactions vs direct API baseline.

AE note: seat of influence was CTO; CTO neutral — SRE favored OpenAI for operational simplicity.

Competitive intel: openai rep provided simplified migration plan and volume discounts; influential.

No formal RFP; decision tracked as 'vendor consolidation to OpenAI'

title: Blueharbor ModelWorks
preview: Lead: Head of ML Ops = Priya Desai. Use-case originally: production reranking for ad-serving + embeddings for creative search. High latency sensitivity (p95 < 60ms for rerank path), throughput ~800 qps during peak. Preferred dedicated pool + VPC peering. Security heavy: mandatory SSO (SAML), KMS-backed key managemen...
text:
Lead: Head of ML Ops = Priya Desai. Use-case originally: production reranking for ad-serving + embeddings for creative search. High latency sensitivity (p95 < 60ms for rerank path), throughput ~800 qps during peak. Preferred dedicated pool + VPC peering. Security heavy: mandatory SSO (SAML), KMS-backed key management, SOC2 required, logs retention 2 years, EU data residency for some customer segments.

Conversation style: choppy, internal freeze. Key quote on 2026-02-12 call: "We're not hiring this quarter — everything's on ice until headcount reopens."

Deal history: strong technical fit; procurement liked deterministic pricing but CFO pulled back. Multiple exec-level reprioritizations (martech consolidation). AE attempted exec intro to CTO in Jan — delayed. SE left draft rollout plan with staged capacity (can start with smaller dedicated slab). Offered 90-day pilot credits (discount...

text:
Status: STALLED — account went quiet after discovery.

Quick recap: Apexhaven is a mid-market ecommerce platform (personalization + conversational support). Primary ask: replace current ensemble (open-source + in-house) with a predictable, lower-latency serving layer that supports reranking and chat at peak traffic (p50 target ~60ms token-level, throughput ~500 concurrent chats). They prefer on-prem/VPC deployment for PII and residency.

Security: CISO escalation — asked for SOC2 evidence, KMS architecture, audit log retention policy, and a redline-able security appendix. Engineering is friendly to Redwood runtime (likes KV cache + batching suggestions) but security team flagged "no third-party key exposure" requirement.

Commercial: expected ARR band ~50k-150k; procurement indicated Q1 budget constraints — possible freeze next quarter. AE pushed Dedicated reserved capacity model. Com...

title: Haven Arc Trust Systems
preview:
Summary: Large defense-adjacent contact center operator evaluating Redwood Private for agent-assist and ticket summarization in a fully air-gapped environment. Primary concern is deterministic, auditable outputs and resilience during surge events.

Key requirements: minimal dependency on outbound network, ability to...
text:
Summary: Large defense-adjacent contact center operator evaluating Redwood Private for agent-assist and ticket summarization in a fully air-gapped environment. Primary concern is deterministic, auditable outputs and resilience during surge events.

Key requirements: minimal dependency on outbound network, ability to run in air-gapped on-prem datacenter, FIPS/HSM KMS support, audit log continuity, SSO via enterprise SAML, retention controls per DoD guidelines. Integration must sit in front of existing Genesys/Avaya ACD and call-routing stack; need tool-calling to surface backend actions (ticket creation, CRM lookups) without exposing external endpoints.

Performance targets discussed on 2026-01-15 call: 600-900 concurrent agents at peak; target 1st-token latency <250ms for agent-assist in most flows; summary generation SLA <500ms average; deterministic reranking for KB hits with explai...
